zh.lifeSummary: 當William de la Spine於大約1270年出生在英國瓦維其可爾時，他的父親William de la Spine II of Coughton38歲，母親Joan de Cocton37歲。他於1299年之前與Margery de Durvassal在英國瓦維其可爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1327年在英國瓦維其可爾庫頓享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de la Spine于大约1270年出生在英国瓦維其可爾时，他的父亲William de la Spine II of Coughton38岁，母亲Joan de Cocton37岁。他于1299年之前与Margery de Durvassal在英国瓦維其可爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1327年在英国瓦維其可爾庫頓享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Spine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (Angus): nickname from Middle English spink ‘chaffinch, finch’ (probably of imitative origin), bestowed on account of some fancied resemblance to the bird. Compare Pink .

variants.name: Pink
meanings: English: nickname for a chirpy person, from Middle English pink(e), pinch (Old English pinc(a)) ‘chaffinch’.
English: possibly a nickname for a physically small person, from an unrecorded Middle English pink.
German: nickname for a blacksmith, from an onomatopoeic word imitating the sound of hammering which was perceived as pink(e)pank.

variants.name: Spinks
meanings: English: variant of Spink , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When William de la Spine was born about 1270, in Warwickshire, England, his father, William de la Spine II of Coughton, was 38 and his mother, Joan de Cocton, was 37. He married Margery de Durvassal before 1299, in Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1327, in Coughton, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de la Spine est né environ 1270, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, William de la Spine II of Coughton, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Joan de Cocton, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Margery de Durvassal avant 1299, à Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1327, à Coughton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de la Spine um 1270 in Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de la Spine II of Coughton 38 und seine Mutter Joan de Cocton 37. Er hat vor 1299 in Warwickshire, England Margery de Durvassal geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1327 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Coughton, Warwickshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de la Spine è nato il circa 1270 nel Warwickshire, England, il suo padre, William de la Spine II of Coughton, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Joan de Cocton, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Margery de Durvassal prima del 1299, nel Warwickshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1327, nel Coughton, Warwickshire, England, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William de la Spineが約1270年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアで生まれた時，父William de la Spine II of Coughtonは38歳，母Joan de Coctonは37歳であった。1299年以前にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアでMargery de Durvassalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1327年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアコウトンで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de la Spine(이)가 대략 1270년 영국 워윅실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de la Spine II of Coughton(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Cocton(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1299년 이전 영국 워윅실르에서 Margery de Durvassal(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1327년 영국 워윅실르 코우그h턴에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de la Spine nasceu aproximadamente 1270, em Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de la Spine II of Coughton, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Cocton, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery de Durvassal antes 1299, em Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1327, em Coughton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de la Spine родился около 1270 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William de la Spine II of Coughton было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Cocton, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery de Durvassal перед 1299 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1327 г. в местности коугтон, варвиккшир, Англия в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de la Spine nació aproximadamente 1270, en Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William de la Spine II of Coughton, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Joan de Cocton, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Margery de Durvassal antes de 1299, en Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1327, en Coughton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años.
